metadata.name: Schedule a Recurring System Task with Cron
metadata.description: Schedule a recurring system task by using the system crontab file in the Cron directory
objectives: Schedule a recurring system job to count the number of active users

tasks.name: Verify usercount crontab file exists
tasks.description: The /etc/cron.d/usercount file must exist with the cron job configuration
tasks.failure_message: The usercount crontab file is missing
hints: Create /etc/cron.d/usercount with the required cron job

tasks.name: Verify SHELL variable is set
tasks.failure_message: SHELL variable is not set to /bin/bash
hints: Add SHELL=/bin/bash to the crontab file

tasks.name: Verify PATH variable is set
tasks.description: The crontab must specify the PATH variable
tasks.failure_message: PATH variable is not correctly configured
hints: Add PATH=/sbin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin to the crontab file

tasks.name: Verify MAILTO variable is set
tasks.failure_message: MAILTO variable is not set to root
hints: Add MAILTO=root to the crontab file

tasks.name: Verify cron job runs every 3 minutes
tasks.description: The cron job must be scheduled to run every 3 minutes
tasks.success_message: Cron job is scheduled to run every 3 minutes
hints: This means every 3 minutes, all hours, all days

tasks.name: Verify cron job uses logger command
tasks.description: The cron job must use the logger command to log active user count
tasks.success_message: Cron job correctly logs active user count
tasks.failure_message: Cron job command is not correctly configured
hints: The job should run as root user
Use: logger "There are `w -h | wc -l` active users"
Full line: */3 * * * * root logger "There are `w -h | wc -l` active users"
